title: Ensure no VPC security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0  to port 22

input_row.title: Ensure no VPC security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0  to port 22
input_row.description: VPC security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Virtual Servers. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 22.
input_row.rationale: Removing uncontrolled connectivity to remote console services, such as SSH, reduces a server's exposure to risk.
input_row.audit: Perform the following tasks to determine if the account is configured as prescribed: 1. Login to the IBM Cloud Portal at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. At the Menu icon, select VPC Infrastructure-->Security Groups. 3. For each security group, perform the following: a. Select the security group name. b. Ensure no Inbound Rule exists that has a value that includes port 22 and has a Source of 0.0.0.0/0. Note that a port range value of ALL or a port range that includes port 22, e.g. 0-1024, are inclusive of port 22.
input_row.remediation: 1. Login to the IBM Cloud Portal at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. At the Menu icon, select VPC Infrastructure-->Security Groups. 3. For each security group, perform the following: a. Select the security group name. b. Identify the Inbound rule to be removed. c. Using the Options icon, select Delete.
input_row.impact: For updating an existing environment, care should be taken to ensure that administrators currently relying on an ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 have access to port 22 through another, more restrictive, security group.
input_row.default_value: Unless modified, the default security group includes a rule with protocol set to TCP, a value of 22 and a Source of 0.0.0.0/0.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit control specifies checking VPC security groups for ingress rules that allow connections from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 22. This type of configuration is standard in IBM Cloud and can be queried using IBM Cloud APIs.
- The description provides specific details about the requirement, and the audit instructions outline steps that can be automated.
- IBM Cloud APIs have capabilities for listing security groups and their rules.
- The audit mentions reviewing VPC security groups, which is exposed via the IBM Cloud CLI and APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_vpc_security_group_ingress_restriction_port_22

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the IBM Cloud CLI command `ibmcloud is security-groups` to list all security groups in the specified region.
- For each security group, use `ibmcloud is security-group <security-group-id>` to retrieve the specific inbound rules.
- Check for any inbound rules where the "port" equals 22 and the "source" equals "0.0.0.0/0".
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** No inbound rules found with port 22 and source 0.0.0.0/0.
  - **Fail:** At least one inbound rule exists with port 22 and source 0.0.0.0/0.
- If using the IBM Cloud API directly, use `/v1/security_groups` endpoint to enumerate security groups and `GET /v1/security_groups/{id}` to inspect their rules.

This approach efficiently verifies compliance with the specified audit control using automation, reducing the need for manual checks and minimizing human error.
